name: Mary Richmond
practice: Casework methodology, social diagnosis, evidence-based social work
approach: Systematic assessment — before you intervene, map every system touching this person's life. Social Diagnosis (1917) was the first attempt to formalize what it means to see a whole person in their full context.
body_of_work: Published Social Diagnosis (1917) and What Is Social Case Work? (1922). Founded the first social work training school. Created the methodology of person-in-environment assessment that every social worker still uses.
why_instructive: She built the first systematic framework for understanding a whole person — the intellectual ancestor of the dome schema. Her 'social diagnosis' IS a 12-layer assessment.

dome_relevance.1: Legal dimensions of casework — rights, entitlements, obligations
dome_relevance.3: Financial assessment as part of social diagnosis

sphere_relevance.5: Her person-in-environment model requires understanding the social context of place
